title: 10 best herbal creams for dry mouth

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with dry mouth because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. This helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort. The licorice root also stimulates saliva production, which can help to replenish moisture and promote a healthy oral environment. As a result, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief for individuals experiencing dry mouth, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth and throat.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with dry mouth by acting as a natural demulcent, providing a protective barrier to soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.

Licopyranocoumarin: It helps with dry mouth by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and irritation of the mucous membranes, thereby alleviating the symptoms of dry mouth.
Icariin: It helps with dry mouth by acting as a vasodilator, which can help increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation of the mucous membranes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and moisturize the mouth, reducing dryness and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used in creams to provide a gentle and calming effect, helping to reduce irritation and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Extract the roots of glycyrrhiza glabra using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a ratio of 1:5 at room temperature for 2 hours.
Filter the extract through a 0.2-micron filter to remove impurities and obtain a clear liquid.
Combine the filtered extract with a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:3 and heat the mixture gently.
Blend the mixture with beeswax and candelilla wax in a ratio of 1:1 to create a stable emulsion.
Fill the blended mixture into containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with dry mouth because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which work together to calm and hydrate dry mouth tissues. Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce redness and irritation, providing quick relief from dry mouth discomfort. Regular use of Aloe vera creams can lead to improved oral health and a fresher, more comfortable feeling.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and reduce inflammation in the mouth, alleviating symptoms of dry mouth.
Barbaloin: Barbaloin has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, reducing the risk of infections and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Chrysophanic acid: Chrysophanic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect the oral mucosa from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of dry, irritated tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are used due to their high gel content, which provides soothing and moisturizing properties for dry mouth.
Gel from Leaves: The gel is used for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in dry mouth.
Gel from Inside Leaves: The clear inner gel from the leaves of Aloe vera is used for its moisturizing and hydrating properties, helping to soothe and calm dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 100g aloe vera gel 1 tablespoon coconut oil and 10g beeswax.
Weigh 50g aloe vera gel and mix with 1 tablespoon coconut oil in a small bowl.
Combine the aloe vera mixture with 10g beeswax in a double boiler and heat.
Blend the mixture until it thickens and reaches a temperature of 120 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool before applying to the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with dry mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work together to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth. This leads to reduced inflammation and improved moisture levels, helping to alleviate dry mouth symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects may also contribute to its beneficial impact on oral health, promoting a healthy balance of saliva and moisture in the mouth.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with dry mouth by stimulating saliva production, thereby alleviating xerostomia.
Caffeic acid derivatives: These phenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation associated with dry mouth, allowing for better oral hydration.
Triterpene saponins: These compounds may have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for dry mouth due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe dry, irritated tissues.
Roots: They are used to make creams for dry mouth due to their immunomodulatory effects and ability to stimulate saliva production.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for dry mouth due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect dry, irritated tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea leaves with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a blender.
Blend the mixture at high speed for 30 seconds to create a smooth paste.
Mix the blended paste with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter to create a creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita creams helps with dry mouth because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mouth. By calming irritated tissues and reducing inflammation, chamomile creams can help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with dry mouth. Additionally, chamomile has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to dry mouth.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and calm the oral mucosa, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with antioxidant properties, which can help protect the oral mucosa from oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of dry, irritated tissues.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to calm and reduce inflammation in the oral mucosa, providing relief from dry mouth discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The most used part of Chamomilla recutita in creams for dry mouth is the flowers, as they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm and moisturize the mouth and lips.
Seeds: The seeds of Chamomilla recutita are also used in creams for dry mouth, as they contain essential oils that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
Leaves: The leaves of Chamomilla recutita are used in creams for dry mouth due to their soothing and calming properties, which help to reduce irritation and discomfort in the mouth and lips.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried flowers and stems of chamomilla recutita, then sift them through a fine mesh.
Steep the sifted flowers in 1000 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 14 days, shaking daily.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix 30 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of candelilla wax, and 10 grams of vitamin e oil into the oil mixture.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes, then allow it to cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with dry mouth because they contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth. The cream's ability to lock in moisture and promote a healthy oral environment helps to reduce dryness and discomfort associated with dry mouth. Additionally, Calendula's antiseptic properties help to combat bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate dry mouth symptoms, promoting a balanced oral ecosystem and relief from dry mouth. This leads to improved oral health and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help with dry mouth by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of oral tissues, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Phenolic acids: Help with dry mouth by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth and prevent infections that can exacerbate dry mouth symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Help with dry mouth by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth from damage and promote the healing of oral tissues.
remedy_parts: Flowers: These are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis in creams for dry mouth, as they are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis are also used in creams for dry mouth due to their ability to provide moisture and soothe irritation, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort.

Seeds: The seeds of Calendula officinalis are used in creams for dry mouth because they contain oils that can help to lock in moisture and provide protection to the mucous membranes, reducing dryness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of dew for best potency.
Dry the flowers in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Blend the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of emulsifying wax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams helps with dry mouth because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The herb, also known as lemon balm, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the oral cavity. Its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties also help combat the bacteria that can contribute to dry mouth. Additionally, Melissa officinalis creams can help stimulate saliva production, which can help to alleviate dry mouth symptoms. This makes it a popular natural remedy for dry mouth.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps to reduce inflammation and improve saliva production, thereby alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
Luteolin: This flavonoid exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the reduction of oral inflammation and promotion of saliva production.
Bornyl acetate: This terpene has a stimulating effect on the salivary glands, increasing saliva production and helping to alleviate dry mouth discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds and have been traditionally used to soothe and calm dry mouth symptoms.
Flowers: They are a rich source of melissic acid and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to alleviate dry mouth.
Buds: They are often used in herbal remedies due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and combat dry mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Infuse the leaves in 250ml of hot water for 10-15 minutes to create a herbal liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax and shea butter melt completely.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour it into a clean glass container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with dry mouth because of its anti-inflammatory properties and high water content, which helps to soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes in the mouth. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains antioxidants and vitamins, including vitamin E and vitamin C, that aid in promoting saliva production and reducing inflammation, thereby alleviating dry mouth symptoms. This natural remedy provides instant relief and helps to restore the mouth's natural moisture balance.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help to moisturize and soothe the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with dry mouth.
Anthraquinones: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth and throat, alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
Vitamins (especially vitamin E): Acts as an antioxidant, which can help to protect the mucous membranes from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy oral mucosa and alleviating dry mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of aloe vera gel, which is rich in soothing and moisturizing properties that help to hydrate and calm dry mouth.
Gel from Leaves: Also known as aloe vera juice, it's used in creams to provide anti-inflammatory and antibacterial benefits that help to heal and protect the oral mucosa.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 500 grams of aloe barbadensis gel, which has been extracted from the plant's pulp.
Combine the aloe gel with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax and coconut oil melt completely in 10 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and stir in 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of distilled water.
Pour the mixture into a sterile container and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with dry mouth because they contain compounds that stimulate saliva production, helping to keep the mouth moist and comfortable. The anti-inflammatory properties of the herb also reduce swelling and irritation in the mouth, further alleviating dryness. This natural remedy provides relief from dry mouth, also known as xerostomia, which can be caused by various factors such as dehydration, medications, or certain medical conditions. It offers a soothing and effective solution for a dry, uncomfortable mouth.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps to stimulate saliva production, reducing dryness in the mouth.
Alkaloids: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to reducing inflammation and irritation associated with dry mouth.
Mucilages: Forms a protective barrier, soothing and moisturizing the mucous membranes in the mouth, helping to alleviate dryness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in mucilage, a soothing agent that helps to provide relief from dry mouth by forming a protective barrier.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale are another source of mucilage, making them a valuable ingredient for dry mouth creams.
Rhyzomes: Rhizomes of Symphytum officinale also contain mucilage, which contributes to their use in dry mouth creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers for later use.
Steep 10 grams of dried symphytum officinale leaves in 100ml of carrier oil at 60-70°c for 2 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container discard solids.
Combine the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax.
Mix the blend thoroughly and pour into a mold to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with dry mouth because they contain menthol, a natural compound that stimulates saliva production. This helps to increase the flow of saliva, which in turn helps to keep the mouth moist and comfortable. The cooling sensation provided by menthol also helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dry mouth. Additionally, menthol has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, further reducing the risk of oral infections.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with dry mouth by creating a soothing, cooling sensation on the mucous membranes, which can temporarily alleviate dryness and discomfort.
Limonene: Limonene has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth, thereby alleviating dry mouth symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, has antioxidant properties that help protect the mucous membranes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for dry mouth due to their high menthol content, which helps to numb and soothe the mouth.
Roots: Used to make creams for dry mouth due to their antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and 50g of fresh flowers in the morning.
Combine the harvested leaves and flowers with 250g of distilled water and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 5 minutes of straining.
Add 50g of vegetable glycerin and 100g of candelilla wax to the strained liquid and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with dry mouth because they contain Tea Tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth. This can help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties can help to hydrate the mouth, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with dry mouth. Regular use may also help to reduce the risk of infections and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe dry mouth by its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalool: Exhibits soothing and calming effects, which can help to reduce discomfort and dryness associated with dry mouth.
Triptolide: Demonstrates antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi in the mouth, contributing to a decrease in dry mouth symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melaleuca alternifolia leaves are commonly used in creams for dry mouth because they contain essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Stems: Melaleuca alternifolia stems are used in creams for dry mouth because they contain the essential oils responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.

Leaves: (repeated as the second most common part) Melaleuca alternifolia leaves are also used in creams for dry mouth because they contain antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that help soothe and protect oral tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40% of the mature leaves of melaleuca alternifolia in the early morning, after dew has evaporated.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for 2-3 hours to obtain an essential oil with a minimum yield of 2%.
Combine 20% of the essential oil with 80% of a base oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean container.
Heat the mixture gently for 10-15 minutes while stirring constantly, then allow it to cool and thicken.
Whip the cooled mixture until it reaches the desired consistency and texture for a herbal melaleuca cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry mouth are topical treatments derived from plants, which help alleviate dryness and discomfort in the mouth by providing moisture and soothing the mucous membranes. The best herbal creams for dry mouth include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe vera, Echinacea purpurea, Chamomilla recutita, Calendula officinalis, Melissa officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Symphytum officinale, Mentha x piperita, and Melaleuca alternifolia. If left untreated, dry mouth can cause difficulty swallowing, speaking, and eating, leading to social isolation, malnutrition, and a reduced quality of life.
